title: clermontia peleana subsp. singuliflora
intro: Clermontia peleana subsp. singuliflora is a rare and endangered flowering plant species native to Hawaii, known for its single white flowers and purple bracts. This plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Clermontia peleana subsp. singuliflora include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its ability to act as an antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, teas, and tinctures, although its use is largely anecdotal due to its limited availability and the need for further scientific research.
